methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: World Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Nominal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Photoroom is a Series B-stage AI & Software company founded by Matthieu Rouif and Eliot Andres, currently valued at $500M post-money after raising $43M to date. The peer universe comprises 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Palantir), with same-sector, same-stage private peers including Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, and Nominal.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $500M last post-money valuation, yielding $950M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $5.3B, with a range spanning $1.0B (Nominal) to $32.0B (Safe Superintelligence).

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $808M (low) to $6.1B (high), with a median of $3.1B. The substantial spread reflects meaningful dispersion within the peer set—the 32x range between minimum and maximum comp valuations. The current $500M valuation sits below the stage-progression implied value and well below peer median, suggesting either a discount to the comp universe or material differences in growth trajectory, unit economics, or market position relative to peers. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the peer set given heterogeneity in AI subsector focus and the timing and magnitude of revenue inflection.
